posts.body: What do you do with gold coin after you reserve a card? Can it only be used with that card or does it become a 'wild' coin and be used as you wish?

Dezzz wrote:

What do you do with gold coin after you reserve a card? Can it only be used with that card or does it become a 'wild' coin and be used as you wish?

The second one.

rkonigsberg wrote:

Dezzz wrote:

What do you do with gold coin after you reserve a card? Can it only be used with that card or does it become a 'wild' coin and be used as you wish?

The second one.

Right.  And remember, a player can still reserve a card when there are no gold chips in the bank, as long as the player's reserve card total is no greater than three.
Tx. Can't find the answer in the rules.

The following is from the 2014 Space Cowboys edition of Splendor:

(under Reserve a development card)
Reserving a card is also the only way to get a gold token (joker).  If there is no gold left, you can still reserve a card, but you won't get any gold.

Buying a development card
To purchase a card, a player must spend the number of tokens on the card.  A joker token can replace any color. The spent tokens (including any jokers) are returned to the middle of the table.

We always played that you could only use it as a joker for that card because our instructions showed the coin placed on the card to reserve it.we had many 'family discussions' as to how to interpret the rule. TX for the clarification.
That one I didn't know! That helps.

Dezzz wrote:

We always played that you could only use it as a joker for that card because our instructions showed the coin placed on the card to reserve it.we had many 'family discussions' as to how to interpret the rule. TX for the clarification.

It's important to keep the gold tokens together with your other tokens to ensure that you don't accidentally exceed the limit of ten tokens.
Picture examples complement rules, don't overwrite them, always read the rules as first source.

Eldard wrote:

Dezzz wrote:

We always played that you could only use it as a joker for that card because our instructions showed the coin placed on the card to reserve it.we had many 'family discussions' as to how to interpret the rule. TX for the clarification.

It's important to keep the gold tokens together with your other tokens to ensure that you don't accidentally exceed the limit of ten tokens. 

We have always kept our colored tokens on top of our same-colored cards, in five side-by-side columns. And we have kept our gold tokens on top of our face down reserve cards (in a sixth stack to the right of our colored cards). That way they are all in a line, and the tokens are easy to count at a glance. And since like colored tokens are kept on top of like colored cards, it is easy to see our total buying power at a glance as well.

I suspect many players organize their chips and cards in a similar way.

EDIT to add... That's not to say that the gold coins can *only* be used on the Reserve cards (they can be spent on *any* card purchase as has been pointed out above). That's just how we organize them so that they are easy to count.  

Cheers!

That works.  I always keep my tokens with the like colored cards and in line abreast, and the gold tokens in line with them.  Whatever works. 🙂
